expectations.text: Flags every any usage in the file
expectations.evidence: Issue 1 flags all four any locations: data param, result variable, getConfig return, formatUser param.
Issue 2: flags both 'data as UserData' (line 16) and 'user.role as ...' (line 40) as hollow casts.
Issue 7: 'Default export of a named function. Default exports make auto-imports less predictable.'
Issue 6: 'Two boolean parameters create four behavioral permutations. Callers cannot understand the function's behavior.' Recommends options object.
Issue 3: 'The ! operator suppresses TypeScript's null check. config will frequently lack a settings property entirely.'

expectations.text: Does NOT flag formatting or import order issues
expectations.evidence: No formatting, indentation, or import order complaints in the review.

assertion_critique: All 6 assertions pass for both with-skill and without-skill. The code review assertions are non-discriminating -- the model catches these violations without the skill.

Qualitative difference not captured: with-skill maps each finding to specific standard sections, uses the skill's confidence framework (high/moderate), and identifies the parallel type definition drift. Without-skill is less structured.
